meta.group_name: ABC Annapoorna Base Camp October 24 (now: Kananaskis Hike)

past_hikes.name: Machu Picchu Trek
past_hikes.notes: Group completed the Inca Trail to Machu Picchu. Max elevation ~14,000 ft. Altitude medicine (Diamox) was taken. Used as benchmark for ABC preparation. T-shirts with trail distances were souvenirs from Alpaca.
past_hikes.training_value: High – confirmed ability to reach 14k ft with medication

past_hikes.name: Havasupai Falls Backpacking Trip
past_hikes.notes: 4-day trip averaging 11 miles per day. Extremely beautiful. Mule service used to carry heavy gear. Very scenic waterfalls.

past_hikes.name: Flagstaff Training Hikes (Humphreys, Elden, Kendrick)

peaks.name: Mount Elden
peaks.notes: Start from main parking lot, summit and back. Lung buster.

peaks.name: Humphreys Peak
peaks.notes: Highest peak in AZ. Slow above 11k ft. Saddle section is very challenging.

past_hikes.notes: Training trip specifically to prepare for ABC Nepal. All trails rated Hard. Max elevation 12,800 ft at Humphreys – comparable to ABC's 13,750 ft. Diamox available for anyone feeling altitude effects. Participants: Bala, Kannan, Srikumar, Sam Raja, Binoj (flew in from out of town), Giridhar (post-surgery, limited).

past_hikes.name: Boundary Waters Backpacking Canoe Trip
past_hikes.location: Boundary Waters Canoe Area, Minnesota, USA
past_hikes.notes: Backpacking + canoe trip. No phone access throughout. Bala and Sam Raja attended.

past_hikes.name: Annapurna Base Camp (ABC) & Poon Hill Trek
itinerary: Day 1: Pokhara → Jhinu Danda by jeep → 2.5h hike to Chhomrong (2170m)
Day 3: Trek to Machhapuchhre Base Camp MBC (3700m), 5–6 hrs
Day 4: 2h early morning walk to ABC (4130m) for sunrise. Trek back to Bamboo, 5–6 hrs
Day 8: Hike to Poon Hill viewpoint (3120m) for sunrise. Trek to Tikhedhunga, drive to Pokhara
included_in_tour: Land transportation to/from trek start and end
Welcome and goodbye dinner – Nepalese family
past_hikes.notes: 8-day route via Ghandruk approach, returning via Poon Hill/Ghorepani. Tea house accommodation throughout. Sherpas carried main bags (max 12.5kg per person). Hikers carried small day packs. Sleeping bags rentable in Pokhara (250 Rs/day). Trekking poles rentable (100 Rs/day). Walking slowly above snow line critical. Temperature: daytime 24–30°C at lower altitudes; MBC/ABC can drop to -10°C at night.

past_hikes.name: Cactus to Clouds (San Jacinto via Palm Springs Aerial Tramway)
past_hikes.location: Palm Springs / Mount San Jacinto, California, USA
past_hikes.notes: Gopa Ganesan organized and created a detailed Excel packing/time plan per mile. Referenced as 'Gopa's excel spreadsheet was a masterpiece of hike planning'. Plan shared with Adi (another member) for their attempt in May 2026.
resources: Gopa's mile-by-mile Excel timing spreadsheet shared in group

past_hikes.name: Mount Wrightson
past_hikes.location: Santa Rita Mountains, south of Tucson, Arizona, USA
past_hikes.notes: Day hike starting at ~5,000 ft, summiting at 9,500 ft. 3,500 ft elevation gain. Described as 'kick ass' cardio workout. Good leg and lung builder at lower base elevation than Flagstaff.

upcoming_hikes.name: Kananaskis / Banff Hiking Trip
upcoming_hikes.notes: Deposits of $480 per person paid. Multiple group members confirmed. Bala organizing. Srikumar and Binoy Warrier dropped out; Gopa Ganesan and potentially Adi took their spots.

upcoming_hikes.name: Patagonia W Trek (Torres del Paine)
upcoming_hikes.location: Torres del Paine National Park, Patagonia, Chile
upcoming_hikes.notes: Total trip cost ~$5,000–$6,000+ per person including airfare and misc. Operator confirmed reservation for 10 people. Price is ~$1,500 higher than previous year quotes. Binoj Kitchu interested in organizing Spiti Valley India trip for next summer.

upcoming_hikes.name: The Enchantments Traverse
upcoming_hikes.notes: Shared by Gopa Ganesan in June 2026. No dates or deposits yet.

upcoming_hikes.name: European Hut-to-Hut / Inn-to-Inn Routes
upcoming_hikes.location: France, England, Scotland, Iceland, Spain, TMB Switzerland
upcoming_hikes.notes: Mentioned by Bala as strong future options. TMB (Tour du Mont Blanc) from Switzerland specifically highlighted.

upcoming_hikes.name: Spiti Valley
upcoming_hikes.notes: Binoj Kitchu suggested organizing this for next summer after Patagonia.

official_guide_list: Small day pack (carry yourself during trek)
Waterproof hiking boots with ankle support
Trekking poles (rentable in Pokhara at 100 Rs/day)
Medicines: pain relief, cold/fever, altitude sickness (Diamox recommended)
Rain gear / rain jacket (provided by guide)

rental_in_nepal.other_gear: Most trekking and climbing gear available in Pokhara or Kathmandu
rental_in_nepal.note: Guide recommends renting locally – much cheaper than buying abroad

packing_tips: Sherpas carry main bag – limit to 12.5 kg per person (3 Sherpas for 6 hikers, max 25kg/Sherpa)
Excess luggage can be left at Pokhara hotel
Small hydration day pack carried by yourself
45L pack sufficient for most hikers (Sam Raja confirmed)
Check Buddha Air carry-on policy (may need to check in larger bags)
No back pocket on shorts recommended (avoids spine misalignment)
Hot-wash all gear before returning home (leeches/bed bugs possible in trail accommodations)
Can do laundry at tea houses (hand wash or outsource; allow 1+ day to dry)
Carry ~$200 USD worth of Nepalese Rupees cash for personal expenses

products_to_buy.item: Tailwind Nutrition Endurance Fuel – Mandarin, 50 Servings
products_to_buy.description: Electrolyte & hydration sports drink mix powder. Gluten-free, vegan.
products_to_buy.notes: Giridhar used 1 salt/electrolyte tab per hour during training.

products_to_buy.description: High-performance waterproof hiking boots, discounted during sale.
products_to_buy.notes: Sam Raja purchased these. Great deal at the time.

products_to_buy.description: Waterproof hiking boots used by both Giridhar and Bala.
products_to_buy.notes: Must be broken in before the hike to avoid toe injuries. Break in over multiple hikes.

products_to_buy.item: The North Face Summit Series Offtrail TR Shoes
products_to_buy.notes: Shared as a high-performance trail shoe option.

products_to_buy.description: Lightweight, water-repellant, breathable, quick-dry. Drawstring waist. No back pocket (good for spine). Separate zipper pocket.
products_to_buy.notes: Sold out quickly in sizes M/S at local Sam's Club. Check online or Mesa Sam's. Blue Eddie Bauer was also liked. Khaki Eddie Bauer is Giridhar's regular go-to.

products_to_buy.description: Water-repellant hiking pants and boots seen at Sam's Club.

products_to_buy.item: Hyke & Byke Duck Down Mummy Backpacking Sleeping Bag – 0°F
products_to_buy.description: 0°F rated mummy sleeping bag. 13 inches long, 8 inches diameter when packed.
products_to_buy.notes: Sam owns this and brought it for the Nepal trip. Compact enough to carry.

products_to_buy.item: Goose Down Ultralight Mummy Backpacking Sleeping Bag – 800 FP, rated to -17°C / -30°F
products_to_buy.description: Ultralight 800-fill-power goose down mummy bag. Rated -15°F to -30°F.
products_to_buy.notes: Higher-rated option for very cold nights at MBC/ABC. Overkill for most nights but safe.
To keep gear dry in rain and river crossings.

products_to_buy.description: Prescription medication recommended by guide for altitude sickness prevention.
products_to_buy.notes: Used successfully on Machu Picchu. ABC reaches 13,750 ft – similar to Humphreys. Not mandatory but strongly suggested, especially for first-time high-altitude hikers. Consult a doctor before use.
Good time to buy backpacks, boots, and hiking clothing at a discount.

tips: Train on Humphreys Peak (12,800 ft) in Flagstaff multiple times before any high-altitude trek.
ABC (13,750 ft) is similar altitude to Pikes Peak – manageable without full acclimatization week if you pace yourself.
Walk very slowly above the snow line to prevent altitude sickness.
Take Diamox (consult doctor). Bala used it successfully on Machu Picchu.
Electrolyte tabs or Tailwind Nutrition powder help – 1 per hour during strenuous hikes.
Arrive in Kathmandu at least 1–2 days before hiking for initial acclimatization.
Do at least 1 long hike per weekend in the months leading up to a major trek.
Increase to 2–3 hikes per weekend as trip date approaches.
Target 10 miles / 2,500 ft elevation gain per day on training hikes.
Flagstaff (Humphreys + Elden + Kendrick) is the ideal 3-day training block – plan a trip there.
Mt. Wrightson (south of Tucson) is excellent: 10.6 mi RT, 4,000 ft gain, 9,500 ft peak.
Piestewa Peak in Phoenix is useful for short intense cardio – Elden is ~2X harder.
Mormon Trail in Phoenix usable for flat-speed training.
Deem Hills hiking group organizes Sunday morning group hikes – good community training.
Break in hiking boots over multiple hikes before the trip. Merrell boots especially need this.
Limit main bag to 12.5 kg when Sherpas are provided (3 Sherpas for 6 hikers).
A 45L pack is sufficient; 75L is overkill for 8-day tea house trek.
Rent sleeping bags and poles in Pokhara – much cheaper (250 Rs/day bag, 100 Rs/day poles).
Bring your own sleeping bag for hygiene reasons if you can (rental bags may not be washed between uses).
Water-repellant, lightweight, quick-dry pants essential above snow line.
Hydration pack / bladder for day pack is useful.
Pack a small diary/book – tea houses have no entertainment in evenings.
Carry a headlamp even on day hikes.
Fly Phoenix → Kathmandu. Options: American/Qatar via Doha, United/Singapore via Singapore.
Internal flight KTM ↔ Pokhara: Buddha Air is recommended (~$188 USD or Rs 15,800 booked via Indian agent).
Book Kathmandu–Pokhara flights early – small planes fill up, everyone wants the first flight.
Get SIM card in Nepal – very limited phone signal on trails but power to charge phones everywhere.
Passport-size photo and passport copy required by the guide for trekking permits.
30% deposit (≈$150/person or Rs 12,000) required to book the trek.
Carry ~$200 USD equivalent in Nepali Rupees for personal expenses (drinks, tips, extras).
Excess luggage can be stored at your Pokhara hotel during the trek.
Recommended hotels: Big Pillow Inn (Pokhara), Hyatt Regency (Kathmandu), Hotel Sankar (Kathmandu).
Mountain Everest flight option: airplane ~$225–240 USD per person (1-hour flight close to Everest).
Helicopter tour to EBC: ~$1,200–$1,350 USD per person.

user_recommendations.topic: Tea House Life on the Trail
tips: Tea houses provide blankets but bring or rent a sleeping bag for MBC/ABC nights (-10°C possible).
Dining rooms heated by kerosene stove in tea houses.
Menu at every tea house – Muesli with milk and Tibetan bread are popular breakfasts.
Laundry can be done at tea houses (hand wash yourself or pay to outsource); allow 1 day to dry.
Hot spring at Jhinu Danda on the route – plan a soak.
No alcohol included in trek cost; available to purchase separately.
Hot drinks (tea, coffee, hot chocolate) included in tour cost.
Hot-wash all clothes after returning from Nepal (bed bugs and leeches possible).
Wear masks and avoid handshakes during high-transmission periods.
No back pockets on shorts recommended to keep the spine aligned while hiking.
Walking poles reduce knee stress significantly on descents.
Emergency rescue is not included in tour cost – consider travel insurance with evacuation cover.

itineraries_raw.itinerary_text:
Here is a rough itinerary of the 7 day hike
Day 1 _ Drive by car to Nayapul from Pokhara and trek to Chhomrong ( Hot spring )
Day 2_ Chhomrong to Dovan
Day 3_ Dovan to MBC ( Machhapuchhre Base camp )
Day 4_ MBC to ABC ( about one explore in ABC ) trek Back to Himalaya
Day 5_ Himalya  to Sinuwa
Day 6_ Sinuwa  to Pothana
Day 7_Pothana to Phedi and drive back to Pokhara.
There is a $100 advance for booking

Itinerary :

Day1: Pokhara to Simrong ( 3 Hrs Drive ) . Cross Long suspension bridge . Stop at Jhinu Danda for launch. Hike up hill  by to Chhomrong. 1.3km. 2 hours

Day 2: Trek to Dovan. 3630Mtr. 11.2 
Ktm.5Hrs

Day3: Trek to MBC.3800Mtr.13.5Km.5Hrs

Day4: Trek to ABC.Trek Down to Bamboo. 4130Mtr.2.9Km Up hill. Stop for view and photography. Trek down to Bamboo.13.5Km.6.5Hrs

Day 5: Trek to Sinuwa .11.7Km.5 hours . Another option we can continue walk down to Jhinu Danda to enjoy Hot spring

Day 6: Trek to Pothana.10Km. 1890Mtr .4.5Hrs

Day 7: Pothana to Phedi and Drive Back to Pokhara

Day 6 :  Trek back to Chhomrong.19km . 6 hours .

Day 7 : Trek to Tadapani . 16 km . Trek down to modi khola river and from their hike to Tadapani . Nice green forest walk.

Day 8 : 11km walk to Ghorepani . Many ups and downs . Launch in Deurali. Many Mt views scenery along the way .

We will be following given itinerary :

Day 1 . Pokhara to Jhinu Danda by jeep then 2 and half hours hike to Chhomrong 2170 Mtr

Day 2 . Trek to Dovan 2505 Mtr . 5/6 hours 

Day 3 . Trek to MBC 3700 Mtr 5/6 hours 

Day 4 . Two Hours walk early in the morning to ABC 4130 Mtrs . After Breakfast and photography Trek back to  Bamboo . 5/6 hour

Day 5 . Trek to Chhomrong.5 hours 

Day 6 Trek to Tadapani 2630Mtr. 5 hours 

Day 7. Trek to Ghorepani 5 hours .  2830 Mtr

Day 8. Hike to Poon Hill for View 3120 Mtr . Trek down to Ghorepani after breakfast trek down to Tike Dhunga and drive back to Pokhara

No . Changes are :
1) Start on 7/21 instead of 7/20
2) More distance on Day 2 - by 2 Km and Day 3 - by 6 km 
3) Less distance on Day 4 - by 8 Km

POLL:
Accept proposed itinerary changes- Changes are : 
1) Start on 7/21 instead of 7/20
2) More distance on Day 2 - by 2 Km and Day 3 - by 5.2 km 
3) Less distance on Day 4 - by 8 Km
OPTION: Yes (7 votes)
OPTION: No (0 votes)

Ravi - Rifugio is the hut where we sleep the night before

Day 2 we sleep at Rifugio Peduro and Day 3 we start from there!

Day 8 we sleep at Rifugio Pramperet and Day 9 we start from Rifugio Pramperet and that's the last hike!

That's how I read the itinerary!

Here is the high level itinerary
Day 1 - Leave for Santiago from US
Day 2 - Reach Santiago in the morning and take a flight to Puerto Natales by afternoon. Get to the hotel by evening
Day 3 Start hike, stay at refugio at night
Day 4 Start hike, stay at refugio at night
Day 5 Start hike, stay at refugio at night
Day 6 Start hike, stay at refugio at night
Day 7 Start hike. After completion, return to Puerto Natales.
We can decide what else to do after day 8 as needed <This message was edited>
